name: PGT Custom Windows & Doors
tagline: The freedom to live where and how you want
manufacturing: Primary manufacturing in North Venice, Florida, with additional Florida facilities in Fort Myers, Tampa, and the greater Miami area. PGT also operates plants in Arizona, California, and Utah.
specialty: Impact-resistant aluminum and vinyl windows and doors, including the industry-defining WinGuard® product line.
floridaRelevance: PGT was founded in Florida and helped shape the state's post-Hurricane Andrew building codes. The brand remains America's leading name in hurricane-impact windows and doors.
paragraphs: PGT Custom Windows & Doors traces its roots to 1980, when Paul Hostetler and Rod Hershberger opened a small vinyl fabrication shop in Venice, Florida. What began as VinylTech with three employees grew into the nation's largest manufacturer of impact-resistant windows and doors — a category PGT essentially helped invent after Hurricane Andrew exposed the vulnerability of conventional glazing in 1992.

In 1996, PGT launched WinGuard®, described as the first complete collection of impact-resistant windows and doors from a single manufacturer. Today, millions of WinGuard units have been installed across Florida and coastal markets, with a reputation for meeting the strictest Miami-Dade and Florida Building Code requirements.

PGT manufactures in-house, including glass tempering, laminating, and insulating operations at its North Venice campus. That vertical integration allows tight quality control from raw glass through finished frames — a major reason builders, architects, and homeowners across Florida specify PGT for both new construction and replacement projects.

The company operates under Miter Brands following PGT Innovations' 2024 acquisition, but manufacturing remains Florida-based and product lines including WinGuard, EnergyVue, and architectural systems continue to serve residential, commercial, and high-rise markets.
highlights: Creator of the WinGuard® impact window category
Largest U.S. manufacturer of impact-resistant windows and doors
In-house glass laminating and tempering in Florida

name: ES (ES Windows)
founders: José Manuel Daes and Christian Daes (Energía Solar)
manufacturing: Vertically integrated 6.2 million sq. ft. manufacturing complex in Barranquilla, Colombia, with U.S. offices and showrooms in Miami, Naples, Charleston, New York, and the Florida Panhandle.
specialty: High-performance aluminum architectural windows, doors, curtain walls, and hurricane-resistant systems for residential and commercial projects.
floridaRelevance: ES is one of the highest-volume impact window brands sold in Florida, engineered specifically for High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) requirements and coastal building codes.
paragraphs: ES — formerly marketed as ES Windows — is the residential brand of Tecnoglass Inc., a NYSE-listed manufacturer with more than four decades of glass and aluminum fabrication expertise. The company was founded in 1983 in Barranquilla, Colombia, as Energía Solar before expanding into architectural windows and façades in 1988.

In 2023, ES unveiled a refreshed corporate identity and the tagline "Imagine Extraordinary," reflecting its position as a premium provider of hurricane-resistant and high-thermal-performance window systems. ES products are specified on some of the most demanding residential and high-rise projects in Florida and the Caribbean.

Tecnoglass operates one of the most vertically integrated window manufacturing models in the industry. The Barranquilla campus handles glass processing (including Low-E coating), aluminum extrusion, and finished window assembly — giving ES end-to-end control from raw materials to delivered product.

For Florida homeowners and builders, ES offers a compelling combination of architectural-grade aesthetics, impact certification, and the scale of a global manufacturer. AOA Window Solutions partners with ES to match the right system — from prestige residential windows to commercial curtain wall applications — to each project's performance and design requirements.
highlights: Parent company Tecnoglass listed on NYSE
6.2M sq. ft. integrated plant in Barranquilla, Colombia
U.S. headquarters and showrooms in Florida

name: CGI Windows & Doors
tagline: Strength and security you can trust
manufacturing: Historically manufactured in the Miami area (Hialeah/Medley), serving South Florida and statewide impact markets. Now part of the broader PGT Innovations / Miter Brands manufacturing network.
specialty: Architectural-grade, impact-resistant aluminum windows and doors for residential, multi-family, and commercial applications.
floridaRelevance: CGI was among the first manufacturers to achieve Miami-Dade County impact approval after Hurricane Andrew — helping define what a hurricane window needed to be in the 1990s.
paragraphs: CGI Windows & Doors was founded in 1992, the same year Hurricane Andrew devastated South Florida and triggered the most significant building code reforms in state history. As Florida mandated impact-resistant glazing in the High Velocity Hurricane Zone, CGI emerged as one of the pioneering manufacturers meeting the new standards.

In 1994, CGI became one of the first manufacturers to offer laminated impact glass approved under Miami-Dade County protocols — a landmark achievement at a time when the impact window industry was still being defined. For three decades, CGI built a reputation for architectural-grade aluminum windows and doors serving South Florida's residential and light commercial markets.

Product families including Sentinel, Estate, and Sparta became familiar names on Florida job sites. CGI's focus on clean sightlines, durable aluminum frames, and rigorous impact testing made it a preferred specification for builders and architects in coastal counties.

CGI joined the PGT Innovations family of brands in 2014 and now operates within the Miter Brands portfolio. AOA Window Solutions continues to offer CGI product lines and legacy systems, helping homeowners and property owners access proven impact-rated solutions backed by decades of Florida hurricane performance.
highlights: Pioneer in post-Andrew impact window standards
Part of the PGT Innovations / Miter Brands family

name: JELD-WEN
manufacturing: Global manufacturing across North America, Europe, and Australia — more than 120 facilities in 19 countries, with extensive U.S. production capacity.
specialty: Interior and exterior doors, wood, vinyl, and aluminum windows, wall systems, and related building products for new construction and remodeling.
floridaRelevance: JELD-WEN supplies a broad portfolio of code-compliant windows and doors for Florida residential and commercial projects, including impact-rated and energy-efficient options through its global product lines.
paragraphs: JELD-WEN was founded in 1960 when Richard "Dick" Wendt and four business partners purchased a millwork plant at auction in Klamath Falls, Oregon. From that single facility, the company grew into one of the world's largest manufacturers of doors and windows — now employing approximately 18,000 people across 16 countries.

The company's purpose centers on reliable products that enrich the spaces people live and work in. JELD-WEN designs and manufactures a full suite of interior doors, exterior doors, patio systems, and windows in wood, vinyl, and aluminum — serving both production builders and the repair-and-remodel market.

For Florida projects, JELD-WEN offers products engineered for energy efficiency, durability, and regional code compliance. Brands within the portfolio — including LaCantina® folding door systems and VPI™ windows in North America — give architects and homeowners options across price points and performance tiers.

AOA Window Solutions specifies JELD-WEN where project requirements call for a globally proven manufacturer with deep distribution, consistent lead times, and a wide catalog of styles for custom homes, multi-family, and commercial applications.
highlights: Founded 1960 in Oregon; HQ in Charlotte, NC
One of the world's largest door and window manufacturers
Wood, vinyl, and aluminum product lines

name: Viwinco Windows
tagline: Energy-efficient windows and doors — where production and sustainability go hand in hand
manufacturing: Fully custom vinyl windows and patio doors manufactured at Viwinco's Morgantown, Pennsylvania campus, including an on-site glass facility with laminating and tempering capabilities.
specialty: Custom vinyl windows and patio doors for new construction, replacement, and commercial applications — including impact and non-impact systems.
floridaRelevance: Viwinco serves the Eastern U.S. from the Canadian border through Central Florida, offering both impact-resistant and non-impact vinyl products engineered for demanding coastal conditions.
paragraphs: Viwinco — short for Vinyl Window Company — was founded in 1982 by Jack Barnes and Lou Moretzsohn in Pennsylvania. Still family-owned and operated three generations later, Viwinco has built its reputation on custom manufacturing rather than mass production: every window and door is built to order at its Morgantown facility.

Unlike many national brands that outsource components, Viwinco has brought critical processes in-house — including glass tempering, laminating, and product development. A dedicated 70,000 sq. ft. glass facility added in 2022 expanded impact glass capacity and improved turnaround times for hurricane-rated products.

The company's lean manufacturing approach delivers industry-leading lead times while maintaining tight quality control. Product lines including Edgemont, Cambridge, Signature Series, and OceanView cover double-hung, casement, sliding, and patio door configurations for both impact and non-impact applications.

For Florida customers in Viwinco's service territory, AOA Window Solutions pairs this made-in-USA craftsmanship with local installation expertise — ideal for homeowners who want custom-sized vinyl windows without the compromises of off-the-shelf big-box products.
highlights: Serves Eastern U.S. including Central Florida

name: Origin Windows & Doors
manufacturing: U.S. products designed in the UK and manufactured at Origin's state-of-the-art facility in Venice, Florida (700 Commerce Drive).
specialty: Premium aluminum bi-fold doors, casement windows, and architectural glazing systems with extensive customization options.
floridaRelevance: Origin's U.S. factory in Venice, Florida produces aluminum doors and windows for luxury residential and commercial projects across the Southeast — combining British engineering with local manufacturing.
paragraphs: Origin was established in 2002 in the United Kingdom by cousins Neil Ginger and Victoria Brocklesby, who set out to design and manufacture the highest-quality aluminum bi-folding doors on the market. Their frustration with available products on their own building projects became the foundation for a brand now recognized internationally for precision engineering.

Origin expanded to North America in 2014, opening a full manufacturing facility in Venice, Florida. U.S.-built products include the award-winning Origin Window and a range of bi-fold, sliding, and fixed aluminum systems — each customizable across colors, hardware, glazing, and configuration.

The company's "Your Lead Time, Not Ours" promise reflects a manufacturing model built around made-to-order production rather than warehouse stock. Every Origin product passes rigorous quality testing at the Venice plant before delivery, supporting architects and builders who need exact sizes and premium finishes.

AOA Window Solutions recommends Origin for clients seeking statement-making openings — floor-to-ceiling bi-folds, corner configurations, and slim-profile windows that prioritize natural light and indoor-outdoor living, particularly on high-end Florida waterfront and custom home projects.
highlights: Founded 2002 in the UK; U.S. manufacturing since 2014
Custom aluminum bi-fold doors and windows

name: WinDoor
tagline: Luxury innovations for windows and doors
manufacturing: Custom-manufactured in Orlando, Florida, with products engineered and tested for coastal and high-velocity wind environments.
specialty: Ultra-luxury, custom impact-resistant windows and doors for high-rise condominiums, resorts, hotels, and custom estate homes.
floridaRelevance: WinDoor was founded in Florida exclusively for coastal living — producing some of the largest certified impact openings in the industry for luxury South Florida and statewide waterfront projects.
paragraphs: WinDoor was founded in 2001 in Florida by Frank Lukens and Russ Traficante with a singular focus: high-performance, impact-resistant windows and doors for the luxury market. From five-star resorts and high-rise condominiums to custom estate homes, WinDoor builds every product to specification rather than pulling from a catalog.

The brand's 8100 series sliding door system is widely specified by architects who need oversized openings — pocket doors, 90- and 135-degree corners, and heights up to 12 feet — without sacrificing Miami-Dade or Florida impact certification. WinDoor holds some of the highest impact ratings in the industry.

Innovations including Diamond Glass and Triton Advanced Laminate push impact technology forward with lighter-weight laminated glass that still meets strict hurricane codes. The refreshed brand identity and tagline "Luxury Innovations for Windows and Doors" reflect WinDoor's position as the ultra-premium coastal living specialist within the PGT Innovations family.

PGT acquired WinDoor in 2016, and manufacturing continues in Orlando, Florida. AOA Window Solutions works with WinDoor on projects where standard impact products won't deliver the scale, sightlines, or architectural ambition the design demands.
highlights: Custom luxury impact windows and doors
Part of the PGT Innovations / Miter Brands family

name: Custom Window Systems (CWS)
manufacturing: 700,000+ sq. ft. of manufacturing across Ocala, Miami, Medley, and Jacksonville, Florida.
specialty: Vinyl and aluminum impact-resistant and non-impact windows, doors, and sunroom products engineered for Florida's climate.
floridaRelevance: CWS was the first Florida window manufacturer to earn ENERGY STAR® certification from the NFRC for HVHZ windows — purpose-built for hurricane zones and intense UV exposure.
paragraphs: Custom Window Systems — known as CWS — was founded by Floridians in 1986 with a straightforward mission: build windows and doors specifically for Florida's punishing climate. Nearly four decades later, CWS remains one of the most trusted names in the state's impact and coastal construction markets.

Now a Pella Corporation brand, CWS operates more than 700,000 square feet of manufacturing across Ocala, Miami, Medley, and Jacksonville. Products include vinyl and aluminum impact windows and doors, non-impact systems, and sunroom enclosures — all tested to meet or exceed Florida Building Code and Miami-Dade requirements where applicable.

CWS made history as the first Florida manufacturer to receive an ENERGY STAR® rating from the National Fenestration Rating Council for High Velocity Hurricane Zone windows. That commitment to energy performance alongside storm protection makes CWS a strong specification for homeowners balancing utility costs with hurricane safety.

Real-world performance matters: CWS windows and doors were installed in the "Sand Palace" home in Mexico Beach, which survived Category 4 Hurricane Michael in 2018 while surrounding structures were destroyed. AOA Window Solutions trusts CWS for Florida projects where proven impact performance and dependable delivery timelines are non-negotiable.
highlights: First FL manufacturer with ENERGY STAR HVHZ rating
Vinyl and aluminum impact product lines

name: Euro-Wall
manufacturing: State-of-the-art facility and showroom in North Port, Florida, producing American-made folding, sliding, stacking, and pivot door systems.
specialty: Large-opening aluminum and glass wall systems — folding, multi-slide, stacking, pivot doors, and direct-set windows for residential and commercial projects.
floridaRelevance: Euro-Wall was founded in Florida and engineers every system for hurricane-prone and HVHZ markets, including Miami-Dade approved impact-rated configurations.
paragraphs: Euro-Wall Systems was founded in South Florida with a focus that remains unchanged: build the best large-opening door and glass wall systems for extreme weather. The registered tagline "Superior by Design" reflects a product philosophy centered on engineering, not assembly — every system is built for wind-borne debris, high-velocity hurricane zones, and the daily reality of coastal living.

From its North Port, Florida facility, Euro-Wall manufactures folding (Vista Fold™), multi-slide (Vista Multi Slide™), stacking (Vista Stack™), pivot (Euro Pivot™), and casement window systems. Products meet Miami-Dade and Florida HVHZ standards while offering some of the largest single-panel dimensions available — up to 16-1/2 feet in height or width on select systems.

Thermally broken options including Vista Fold™ and Vista CT™ casement windows support energy code compliance beyond Florida, including California Title 24 and Texas wind-borne debris regions. Despite European-inspired aesthetics, every Euro-Wall product is made in America with automated, high-capacity production for accurate lead times.

AOA Window Solutions specifies Euro-Wall when a project calls for dramatic indoor-outdoor transitions — whole-wall sliding glass, corner folding configurations, or flush-sill multi-slide doors — without compromising the impact rating Florida code demands.
highlights: Founded in Florida; HQ in North Port
American-made folding and sliding wall systems
